Claude code channels destroys open claw. So claw code just released a new update called channels that just beat openclaw its own game. Open claw is one of the fastest growing AI agents in history. And the whole promise is you can control your AI from your phone, text it, give your task, walk away. And anthropic just built that directly into claw code with a new channels update. So there's no complicated install, just your phone, your AI talking to each other. And here's what nobody's putting side by side yet. Today I'm going to show you exactly how to set up Claude Code channels for free in under 10 minutes using Telegram or Discord so that by the end of this video you'll know which tool belongs in your setup. You know how to set it up and you have an AI agent on your phone that you can text task to anytime from anywhere. Let's get straight into it. So this is the channels update as you can see right here and there's basically two links to this right. So you can see here that you can basically set this up on Telegram or Discord. Now they've got full documentation on both via GitHub and also on the website. You can see the details right here. And basically, if you're wondering, okay, what even is Claude Code? To keep it very short, claw code is a tool you install on your computer. It lets an AI Claude write code, manage files, build things for you. You type commands into a terminal window, and Claude does the work. It's incredibly powerful. But there's one big problem. You have to sit there and watch it. The old problem was if Claude was working on something, you had to stay at your computer. The second you walked away, nothing really happened. If you're on your phone, you couldn't send any tasks. If you were asleep, you just waited. You were kind of like locked to your terminal. the new solution with channels, which is what this update is about. Essentially, channels lets you push messages into a running Claude code session from anywhere. So, you want to think of it like giving your AI a phone number. You can text it on Telegram, you can text it on Discord, it reads your message, does the work, and text you back all whilst you're away from your computer. So, it's like giving Claude a walkie-talkie. You press a button, say what you need, and it goes and handles it. So, let's talk about the integration and how to set this up, right? It's working on your your desktop, but it's it's basically like having open claw but in your pocket with claw code, right? Let's talk about this. How is this different from for example something like open claw? That's a great question. So open claw is a separate AI assistant tool and it can run something you deploy itself. It also uses telegram and other chat apps and it's fantastic for controlling AI agent remotely. Now claude code channels is different in a key way. Openclaw runs as his own standalone agent. Claude code channels pushes messages into a claude code terminal session that's already running on your machine. Right? So with clawed code channels, the AI has full access to your computer files. It uses your local tools, your code, your projects. It has the full power of claw code just reachable by phone. Both are powerful. They just solve um slightly different problems. So let's talk about how it works in reality. Right? Here's the simple version. You start claw code on your computer with a a special plugin like we've just done today. A channel like Telegram connects your running session. You send a message on Telegram from your phone. Claw code receives it as an event. Claw code does the task and claw replies back to telegram and then you see the answer on the phone. That's basically it. That's what we've done right here to control claw code from our phone and uh yeah super powerful. Now what you need before you start is claw code installed on your computer claude AI account. You need bun installed a telegram account and also if you're using like a team or enterprise claude then admin needs to turn on channels as well. But that's basically how it works. And then we actually got the full step-by-step instructions inside the AR profer boarding along with a full step-by-step guide on exactly how to implement this. So we actually have like a full framework that we've built where you know I would call this a command and control framework right with claw code channels where this is a framework that makes all of it click. So you want to think of it in three layers. Number one is the brain. So this is core code your AI agent running on your computer. It's the one actually doing the work. Coding, writing, building, automating is always on, always ready and always waiting. Layer number two is the channel. So this is either telegram or Discord. So this is a bridge between your phone and your AI brain. Your message goes in here gets forwarded to claw code and the reply comes back. And then you got layer three, the commander, right? That's you. That's wherever you are sending instructions from your phone like a neural link to your AI agent. Um and when these three layers are connected, you have a fully mobile AI command center. It's just one message, one agent with zero friction. And we actually included a 30-day road map plus a bunch of use cases you use this for.
